editableNewDialogue.text:
Please see the below email draft and check grammar:

"Further to our call, please find attached the requested documents. Should you have any questions, feel free to let us know. Thanks."
Further to our call, please find the requested documents attached. If you have any questions, please feel free to let us know. Thank you.

Also check this one:

"We will keep monitoring the status and provide an update once we heard back."
We will continue to monitor the status and will provide an update once we hear back.

One more:

"Please be advised that the processing time is subject to change without prior notice."
Please be advised that processing times are subject to change without prior notice.
Quick question—should I use “Regards” or “Kind regards” at the end?
Both are acceptable. “Kind regards” can read slightly warmer; “Regards” is a bit more neutral. Either works—choose one and keep it consistent across the email chain.
By the way, I saw a headline about a library digitizing old newspapers—does that help researchers much?

Yes. Digitizing historical newspapers improves access and searchability (OCR), helps preserve fragile originals, and enables large-scale text analysis—useful for historians, genealogists, and social-science research.

Could you please update the email to mention I’ll be out of the office next Thursday afternoon, so they should reach me in the morning instead.

check grammar

Could you please update the email to mention that I will be out of the office next Thursday afternoon, so they should reach me in the morning instead?

Check grammar.
Can you rewrite this email to sound more polite and concise? No grammar check notes this time—just give me the final version.
Sure—please paste the email text you’d like me to rewrite, and I’ll return a polished final version only.
Also, please don’t label anything as “check grammar” in the reply. I’m not doing grammar-check requests anymore; I just want rewrites.
Understood. Share the text, and I’ll provide only the rewritten email without any grammar-check notes or labels.
